title: Vitruvius Module Manifest
description: Structured contract for a Vitruvius module. See ADR 0011.

apiVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Pinned to v1; schema evolution requires a new ADR.

version.description: Semver. Bumped per the module's contract evolution.

owner.description: Team alias matching the Backstage catalog. Not a person name.

description.description: One-line description. Read by Backstage and humans.

semantic.description: Semantic tie to a repo concept (e.g., 'tag-taxonomy-env', 'backstage-component').

avm.description: Azure Verified Modules consumed by this module.

repo.description: Forbidden by ADR 0004. Must be empty. Modules in this repo do not depend on other modules in this repo.

cross_cutting.description: Which cross-cutting concerns this module participates in. See docs/golden-paths.md § 'Defining cross-cutting' for the canonical definition and the rationale for which concerns are on this list.

identity.description: Set true if this module establishes or modifies identity primitives (managed identities, role assignments, federated identity bindings).

observability.description: Set true if this module emits, configures, or hosts observability signals (diagnostic settings, OTel collectors, dashboards). As a module matures it should ship monitoring artifacts in spec.ships.monitoring; experimental modules may carry an empty monitoring list while alerts and dashboards are still being defined. This is a should, not an enforced invariant — JSON Schema cannot express the conditional and no CI check currently enforces it.

secrets.description: Set true if this module manages secret material (Key Vault wiring, rotation handlers, CSI driver integration). See ADR 0009 — secrets are ephemeral by default.

networking.description: Set true if this module shapes network topology or affects traffic flow (VNets, subnets, private endpoints, NSGs, service mesh, APIM).

naming.description: Set true only if this module is the authority on resource names. In practice, only foundation/naming sets this.

tagging.description: Set true if this module establishes or enforces the tag taxonomy. In practice, only foundation/tags sets this. See ADR 0010.

ships.description: Artifacts shipped with the module per ADR 0003.

policy.description: Azure Policy artifacts: definition file names in policy/ or initiative/assignment resource names defined in main.tf (ADR 0003). CI resolves each entry.

monitoring.description: Monitoring artifacts: workbook/dashboard file names in monitoring/ or alert resource names defined in main.tf (ADR 0003). CI resolves each entry.

decisions.description: ADR IDs cited by this module (e.g., ADR-0003).

anti_patterns.description: Anti-pattern IDs this module helps prevent (e.g., AP-001).

examples.description: Examples in examples/ subdirs. CI validates each named example exists.

tests.description: Tests in tests/. CI validates each named test exists.
